title: When to Use a Frontier API
concept: Choose frontier APIs (like Claude-3-Opus) when you need state-of-the-art reasoning, massive context windows, or zero local server setup.

exercises.prompt: What is the easiest way to think about a Frontier API?

options.text: Ready-to-use AI brains in the cloud
Physical computer chips you plug into your laptop

exercises.explain: Frontier APIs are ready-to-use AI brains hosted in the cloud, allowing developers to add advanced intelligence to their apps instantly.

cards.title: Unleash the Giants
cards.body: Have you ever wondered how apps get super-smart AI features overnight without melting your phone? They use Frontier APIs! Let's learn when to plug into these giant brains in the cloud.

cards.title: What is a Frontier API?
cards.body: A Frontier API is a service that lets your app connect to the world's most powerful, state-of-the-art AI models (like Claude-3-Opus) over the internet. You do not need to run the AI on your own computer; instead, you send a message to a cloud server and get a smart answer back.

cards.title: The Gourmet Delivery
cards.body: Think of a frontier API like ordering a meal from a world-class restaurant instead of cooking at home. You do not need to buy stoves, prep ingredients, or wash dishes (no local servers). You just place your order, and a top-tier chef prepares a masterpiece for you immediately.

cards.title: Frontier API in Action
cards.body: Imagine you are building a coding assistant app that needs to find complex bugs in thousands of lines of code. Instead of trying to run a massive AI on your phone, you send the code to a Frontier API like Claude. The API processes the massive amount of code instantly and returns the correct solution to your app.

cards.title: The Custom Model Myth
cards.body: Some people think you must build and train your own AI models to create a powerful app. That is false! With Frontier APIs, you can use the best AI models in the world with just a few lines of code, saving months of work and millions of dollars.

cards.title: Local vs. Frontier API
cards.body: Compare running a model locally on your own machine versus using a cloud-based Frontier API.

right.text: Runs in the cloud. Requires internet and costs per use, but gives you access to the smartest AI models instantly.

left.text: Runs on your device. Free to query, works offline, but limited by your computer's speed and memory.

exercises.prompt: What is one major benefit of using a Frontier API for your app?

options.text: You must buy expensive graphics cards (GPUs).
You don't have to set up or maintain any local AI servers.
Your app will work completely offline without internet.
You get to modify the inner code of the model.

exercises.explain: Frontier APIs are run on cloud servers by companies like Anthropic or OpenAI. You do not need to buy, set up, or maintain any physical servers or expensive GPUs to use them.

exercises.prompt: True or False: You should choose a Frontier API when you need top-tier reasoning and the ability to process very long documents.
exercises.explain: Frontier APIs are perfect for processing huge amounts of text (massive context windows) and solving highly complex logic problems.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word about Frontier API requirements.
exercises.sentence: To use a Frontier API, your application must be connected to the internet.
exercises.explain: Frontier APIs live on cloud servers, so your application must have an active internet connection to send requests and receive answers.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following is powered by a Frontier API?

options.text: A local script sorting files by date.
A Customer Service Bot using Claude-3-Opus to solve a complex billing complaint.
A calculator adding two numbers together.

exercises.explain: The Customer Service Bot is the AI response because it uses a cloud-based Frontier API to analyze and answer a complex customer query. The other options are simple local actions.

exercises.prompt: Your company needs to build a tool that reads 500-page complex legal contracts and answers tricky logic questions about them. You have no local GPU servers and need to launch tomorrow. What is the best strategy?

options.text: Running a small model on your own laptop to save money.
Setting up an offline program that doesn't need internet.
Building a smart chatbot that needs to understand complex legal documents instantly without buying expensive hardware.
Creating a simple script to rename files on your hard drive.

exercises.explain: Using a frontier API gives you access to the world's most powerful models without buying expensive hardware or configuring local servers. However, you must pay for every request you make.
